varieties.name: Satureja montana (Standard Type)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region, Southern Europe (cultivated since Roman times, pre-100 AD)
varieties.traits: Semi-woody perennial, 15-40cm height, 30-45cm spread, dark green leathery needle-like leaves 1-2cm long
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, continuous harvest possible, becomes semi-dormant in winter
varieties.growing_notes: Susceptible to fungal issues in high humidity; good drainage essential. Drought-tolerant once established.
varieties.availability: Mudbrick Herb Cottage (Gold Coast QLD), The Seed Collection Australia, Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Basilea Living Herbs (Brisbane), Pohlmans Nursery (Lockyer Valley QLD)

varieties.name: Satureja montana 'Nana' (Dwarf Winter Savory)
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf form, 10-15cm height, dense mounding habit, smaller leaves than standard type
varieties.yield_notes: Lower total yield but higher per-square-foot productivity due to compact habit
varieties.growing_notes: Slightly better than standard type in humid conditions due to improved air circulation around compact foliage
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, online seed suppliers (True Leaf Market, Baker Creek Heirloom Seeds), may be available through Australian importers

varieties.name: Satureja montana 'Prostrate White'
varieties.traits: Low-growing prostrate habit, 10-15cm height, spreads 30-45cm, trailing stems, coarser leaves than standard
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, spreading habit provides good leaf coverage for harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Good air circulation from spreading habit helps in humid climates
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online suppliers (Mountain Valley Growers, specialty herb catalogues), may require import to Australia

varieties.name: Satureja montana var. citriodora (Lemon Savory)
varieties.origin: Natural variety with lemon-scented foliage, Mediterranean region
varieties.traits: Similar growth habit to standard S. montana, 15-25cm height, aromatic lemon-scented leaves

varieties.name: Satureja montana 'Variegata' (Variegated Winter Savory)
varieties.traits: Cream-edged variegated foliage, compact growth 15-30cm height, ornamental appeal
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than standard type due to reduced chlorophyll
varieties.growing_notes: Similar to standard type but may be slightly more sensitive to stress
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia, specialty ornamental herb nurseries, European seed suppliers

varieties.name: Satureja montana subsp. montana
varieties.origin: Central Mediterranean, widespread wild form (ancient, pre-cultivation)
varieties.traits: Most common form, typical characteristics as described in standard type
varieties.availability: Most commonly available form in Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Satureja montana subsp. variegata
varieties.origin: Eastern Balkans, former Yugoslavia, parts of Italy (regional wild form)
varieties.traits: Variable characteristics, enriched phenolic content, stronger antioxidant properties
varieties.availability: Rare, primarily European botanical collections, limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Satureja hortensis (Summer Savory)
varieties.origin: Southeastern Europe and Asia, Mediterranean region (cultivated since ancient times)
varieties.traits: Bushy annual, 30-60cm height, faster growing than Winter Savory, bronze-green slender leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity during growing season, 8-12 weeks to harvest, complete harvest before frost
varieties.growing_notes: Quick growth outpaces most disease issues, less susceptible to fungal problems than Winter Savory
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, The Seed Collection, Basilea Living Herbs (Brisbane), widely available from Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Satureja montana (Standard Type) - NOT RECOMMENDED
varieties.availability: Not cultivated in Kerala. Online Indian nurseries (Nurserylive, Ayush Kerala) may import on special order but not recommended due to unsuitability.

varieties.name: Summer Savory (Satureja hortensis) - MARGINALLY BETTER BUT STILL POOR
varieties.availability: Limited availability through Indian seed importers, not commonly sold in Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Ocimum tenuiflorum (Tulsi / Holy Basil)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Indian subcontinent, cultivated in Kerala for millennia
varieties.traits: Aromatic perennial herb, 30-60cm height, purple or green leaves, highly aromatic
varieties.availability: Universally available throughout Kerala - every plant nursery, home gardens, traditional suppliers

varieties.name: Coleus amboinicus (Cuban Oregano/Indian Borage)
varieties.origin: Southern and Eastern Africa, naturalized throughout India including Kerala
varieties.traits: Succulent-like herb, large fuzzy aromatic leaves, 30-90cm height, highly heat and humidity tolerant
varieties.availability: Widely available in Kerala nurseries, common in home gardens, traditional medicine shops

varieties.name: Plectranthus amboinicus (Mexican Mint/Country Borage)
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa and Asia, long cultivated in Kerala
varieties.traits: Aromatic succulent herb, thick fleshy leaves, strong oregano-like scent, 30-60cm height
varieties.availability: Common in Kerala gardens, available from local nurseries, traditional medicine suppliers

varieties.name: Mentha spicata (Spearmint)
varieties.origin: Europe and Asia, widely cultivated in Kerala
varieties.traits: Perennial herb, 30-90cm height, aromatic leaves, vigorous spreading growth
varieties.availability: Universally available in Kerala - all nurseries, home gardens, markets

varieties.name: Cymbopogon citratus (Lemongrass)
varieties.origin: Tropical Asia, widely cultivated in Kerala
varieties.traits: Tall grass, 60-180cm height, aromatic citrus-scented leaves, clumping growth
varieties.availability: Extremely common in Kerala - all nurseries, markets, spice plantations

varieties.name: Murraya koenigii (Curry Leaf)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to India, essential Kerala herb
varieties.traits: Small tree/shrub, 2-4m height, pinnate aromatic leaves, intensely fragrant
varieties.availability: Every Kerala garden and nursery, traditional plant essential to Kerala homes
